stocks.name: Butterfly Network
stocks.explanation: Shares surged for a second straight day after Midjourney unveiled a full-body scanner using Butterfly's ultrasound-on-chip technology, marking Midjourney's first hardware product. The co-development deal could generate up to $74 million in payments to Butterfly over five years. Management is amplifying the story with back-to-back conference appearances this week.

stocks.name: Wolfspeed Inc.
stocks.explanation: Rally fueled by a new MOU with GE Aerospace to supply 10 kV silicon carbide MOSFETs for AI, aerospace, and defense applications. Wolfspeed also announced fifth-generation SiC MOSFETs and a new data center solutions team. With ~33% short interest, the stock is prone to sharp short-covering rallies on positive catalysts.

stocks.name: Accenture plc
stocks.explanation: Shares cratered after Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings disappointed on guidance. Revenue rose 6% to $18.7 billion and EPS hit $3.80, but the company cut full-year revenue growth outlook to 3-4% from 3-5%, citing geopolitical tensions and weaker U.S. federal bookings. The guidance cut triggered a broad selloff across IT services stocks.

stocks.name: EPAM Systems
stocks.explanation: Dragged down in the sector-wide IT services selloff triggered by Accenture's guidance cut. Berenberg pulled its Buy rating on Cognizant and flagged AI deflation as a structural headwind for outsourced engineering firms, pressuring the entire group. EPAM is down over 50% year to date on persistent concerns about AI disruption to its core business.

stocks.name: Corning Inc.
stocks.explanation: Continued its AI-infrastructure rally, now up 119% year to date. The stock is benefiting from a multibillion-dollar Amazon contract for optical fiber and connectivity solutions for data center expansion, plus broader momentum from Jensen Huang's Computex commentary on surging demand for high-speed optical connectivity in AI data centers.

stocks.name: Cognizant Technology Solutions
stocks.explanation: Fell to a fresh 52-week low in sympathy with Accenture's guidance cut. Berenberg downgraded the stock, citing AI deflation as a sector-wide theme and concluding Cognizant lacks the differentiated mix to offset the pressure. The company's own fundamentals remain solid — it beat adjusted EPS in each of its last five quarters.

stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: Shares bounced amid renewed interest in sales intelligence platforms as enterprise software deal flow picked up. The stock has been volatile throughout 2026, with investors weighing the company's transition to an AI-powered go-to-market platform against competitive pressures in the B2B data space.
